page: Amazon Web Services r8idb.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by Intel x86_64 processors. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs across 16 physical cores with 2 threads per core, running at a clock speed of 3.9 GHz. The instance is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core.

This instance includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD storage, configured as instance store volumes. It operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps. There are zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses included with this configuration.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the r8idb.8xlarge offers high resource density by combining a large memory footprint with fast local NVMe storage on a dedicated hardware allocation. This configuration is designed for memory-intensive workloads that require low-latency local storage, such as relational databases, in-memory caching systems, and data analytics pipelines.
description: Amazon Web Services r8idb.8xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 32 dedicated vCPUs and 16 physical Intel x86_64 cores running at 3.9 GHz. It is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 8.0 GB of memory per core, and includes 1900 GB of local NVMe SSD instance store storage. Operating on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 33 Gbps. The combination of a high memory-to-vCPU ratio and fast local storage makes this instance highly suitable for memory-intensive applications, database hosting, and high-throughput data processing tasks that benefit from dedicated CPU resources and low-latency storage. This configuration does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses, and it lacks GPU acceleration, focusing its hardware profile entirely on high-density memory and storage performance.
